text.title: python-fastapi Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.symptom: FastAPI emits DeprecationWarning at boot; will break in a future major version.

antipattern.description: Single schema reused for Create + Update + Response.
antipattern.symptom: Clients can set `id` on Create; response leaks `password` field; OpenAPI docs are confusing.
antipattern.root-cause: Shortcut to avoid writing four classes.
antipattern.fix: Define Base (shared), Create (input), Update (partial), Response (output with `from_attributes=True`).

antipattern.symptom: Routes call ORM directly; tests require TestClient; logic not reusable from CLI/cron.
antipattern.fix: Extract into `services/<entity>.py` async function; router calls service, translates result to HTTP.

antipattern.description: Sync DB call inside an async route (e.g. `db.query(...)` with sync Session).
antipattern.symptom: Event loop blocked; concurrency dies; latency spikes under load.
antipattern.root-cause: Mixed sync/async session; agents grab the wrong one.
antipattern.fix: Use AsyncSession + `await db.execute(select(...))`. Verify no sync DB methods in async routes.
